item.title: NU PI™ - A PHENOMENAL MATHEMATICAL DISCOVERY
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://luma.com/qm6jzlh4?tk=r7bV5Z 120 Guests for the NU PI ™ event. Press release info: NU PI™ Ventures Unveils a Verified Geometric Multi-Dimensional Number as the World Comes to Texas for the 2026 Soccer Games. The Houston based company has secured major strategic partnerships with The Premium Advantage®, Xie Xie Media, and Doc-Math.com supporting the worldwide rollout Press Release: https://world.einnews.com/pr_news/916591183/a-number-has-been-found-nu-pi-ventures-prepares-july-2-public-introduction

From Ion District

Friday, July 3, 2026 at 12 AM

Event details

item.title: Ion Networking with Cup of Joey
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

What is the purpose of Cup of Joey? It is to create a connected culture of coffee shop-open networking across our region. Every Friday morning, you’ll have the opportunity to meet new and expand your current networks, talk about tech, inspire innovation, and create community. So, what is the difference between a cup of joe and a Cup of Joe-Y? It is the WHY. It’s not what you do. It is why you do it! At the Ion, these are questions that we want to explore with the tech and innovation community. So, show up, be friendly, and make new connections. Know your why, and we will get to the future together. The future is only a cup away. Register Here: https://www.cupofjoey.org/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Startup Circle Houston 💫
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

Startup Circle Houston 💫 brings together founders, teammates, experts and newbies of startups in Greater Houston! Past topics include: Traction + Funding Stage Identification – What stage are you really at? ICP – Seeking your beachhead market Problem Identification – Love the problem, not the solution Bootstrapping – Knowing your runway and not putting money on fire. 🔥 Founder capacity, 10xing and recruiting for your weaknesses. 🚀 LinkedIn Group: https://www.linkedin.com/groups/16537030/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: How Oil and Gas Professionals are Building Wealth Smarter
item.description:
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/how-oil-and-gas-professionals-are-building-wealth-smarter-tickets-1992002445939?aff=oddtdtcreator&keep_tld=true Join us for an engaging presentation designed for O&G professionals who want to cut through the confusion, and take well informed steps to growing and preserving their wealth. We’ll explore how thoughtful decisions around investing, taxes, and retirement planning can significantly influence your long-term financial outcomes. You’ll learn: – Practical strategies for building a clear and effective financial plan – Four key principles that contribute to a successful retirement – Tax-efficient approaches that may help you retain more of your wealth – Common investment mistakes that can erode long-term results—and how to avoid them Whether you’re early in your career or approaching retirement, this session will help you make more informed financial decisions and gain greater confidence in your path forward.

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: What AI, Cybersecurity, and Tequila Have in Common.
item.description:
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://luma.com/0rfy7te0 Most companies treat AI like a shortcut to growth: it delivers immediate results, but the long-term operational fallout is brutal. ​Everyone is caught up in the rush, plugging automated tools into critical infrastructure without checking the foundation. But speed without security isn’t progress, it’s an open invitation for cyber threats. It’s time to face the structural reality. ​Join Blue People and Alpfa Houston for an unfiltered session with Reynaldo Gonzalez, an industry veteran with 18+ years of experience securing critical infrastructure. ​The Realities We’re Breaking Down: ​AI doesn’t change the value of knowledge; it changes the value of curiosity. The ultimate security edge belongs to leaders who know how to ask the right questions, not just trust the output. ​Your biggest competitive advantage is becoming more human, not less. In a world of automated systems, human judgment and sharp intuition remain the only truly un-hackable firewalls left. ​AI is the greatest operational amplifier ever created. It scales your wins, but it weaponizes your internal flaws. If your baseline security is weak, AI just helps you fail faster and at scale. ​The single biggest risk today isn’t AI, it’s human complacency. The danger isn’t rogue technology or advanced malware; it’s our own blind trust in automated workflows. ​Stop operating on hope. Turn cyber resilience into your unfair advantage. ​📅 July 7th, 2026 | 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM 📍 The Ion Houston Hosted by Blue People & Alpfa Houston ​👉 Ask about our parking options at the end of the event!

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Buying and Selling Businesses
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Ion – Conference Room 029 – 030, 4201 Main St, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Join Lenny Saizan, owner of Murphy Business & Financial – Southwest Houston, Managing Director of Urban Capital Network, and creator of the Legacy Maximizer exit strategy, for a practical, real-world discussion on selling and buying businesses. Selling or acquiring a business is one of the most significant financial decisions most entrepreneurs will ever make, yet many owners and buyers go through the process for the first time without a clear roadmap. This session breaks down the transaction process from both sides of the table and highlights how thoughtful planning, proper valuation, and coordinated advice can materially impact outcomes. For business owners, the discussion centers on how to maximize enterprise value, prepare for a future exit, and align business decisions with personal wealth, tax, and legacy goals. For buyers, the session explores entrepreneurship through acquisition, including how buying an existing business can reduce risk, generate immediate cash flow, and serve as a growth strategy for current operators. Drawing on his experience as a business broker, investment advisor, and financial professional, Lenny connects the technical aspects of M&A with broader exit planning and wealth strategy considerations, helping attendees understand not just how transactions work, but how they fit into a bigger financial picture. Topics include: Current market conditions and buyer demand When to prepare to sell or buy Key drivers of business value How businesses are valued in the real world Recasting and normalizing financial statements Overview of the sales and acquisition process The role of advisors and the deal team Tax, insurance, and planning considerations before and after a transaction This session is ideal for business owners considering a sale now or in the future, prospective buyers exploring acquisition, and professionals who support small business owners through growth and transition. Presenter Lenny Saizan is the owner and lead broker of Murphy Business & Financial – Southwest Houston, where he advises business owners on exit planning, value maximization, and business sales, and supports buyers pursuing acquisitions and franchises. He is also the co-founder and Managing Director of Urban Capital Network, an investment firm focused on expanding access to private investment opportunities. Earlier in his career, Lenny spent two decades in engineering, automation, and oil and gas, leading teams across operational controls, financial controls, and audit. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from Louisiana State University and an MBA from Tulane University. His credentials include Certified Mergers & Acquisition Professional, Six Sigma Green Belt, and Project Management Professional. In 2025, he was recognized as Deal Maker of The Year at Houston Dealmakers conference.  

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Upstream: Digital Tech Meetup at Second Draught
item.description:
Thursday, July 9, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Second Draught, 4201 Main St. Suite 130, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://luma.com/timbergrove Upstream: Digital Tech Meetup – Monthly Social 🍻 No stage. No slides. No panels. Just upstream operators, engineers, founders, and digital leaders getting together over beer and pizza. This is the informal side of the Upstream Digital Tech community. A casual, vendor-light happy hour for people navigating digital change in oil and gas and wanting real conversations. It’s not a presentation. It’s not a pitch night. It’s a room full of people who are actually doing the work. A place to: • Meet others working at the intersection of upstream and technology • Trade field stories, lessons learned, and hard truths • Talk AI, IoT, data, automation, and deployment realities • Build real relationships without a formal agenda Whether you’re deploying technology, evaluating it, building it, or leading teams through it, you’ll fit right in. Come as you are. Bring a friend. Stay as long as you like. 📍 Second Draught @ The Ion District, Midtown Houston 🗓️ Second Thursday of every month | ⏰ 5:30 PM 👉 Reserve your spot. Space is limited.

From Ion District

Event details

Friday, July 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

So, what is the difference between a cup of joe and a Cup of Joe-Y? It is the WHY. It’s not what you do. It is why you do it! At the Ion, these are questions that we want to explore with the tech and innovation community. So, show up, be friendly, and make new connections. Know your why, and we will get to the future together. The future is only a cup away. Register Here: https://www.cupofjoey.org/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Welcome to the Ion: An Introduction and Tour
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Join the Ion’s Mentor in Residence, Wade Pinder, for an engaging tour and introduction to the Ion, Houston’s dynamic hub for innovation and entrepreneurship, powered by Rice University. This event is designed for entrepreneurs, students, professionals, investors, community leaders, and anyone curious about what the Ion has to offer. For those new to Houston and eager to connect with the local innovation ecosystem, this event provides the perfect opportunity to get started. The tour will begin in the lobby at 10:00 AM and conclude at 11:00 AM. During this time, participants will learn about the Ion’s resources, events, and facilities. Highlights of the tour include the technology showcases, coworking spaces, public areas, and the on-site restaurants and bars. About Wade Pinder Wade Pinder is a dedicated advocate for Houston’s innovation community, serving as the Ion’s Mentor in Residence. With a wealth of experience in product management, community building, and fostering entrepreneurial growth, Wade offers invaluable insights into how the Ion serves as a launchpad for ideas and innovation. The Vision of the Ion Powered by Rice University, the Ion is the epicenter of the 16-acre innovation district in the heart of Houston’s midtown. Committed to being the home for Houston’s innovation community, the Ion District propels the transformative culture of people, places, ideas, and experiences that build a better quality of life for all of Houston. The Ion is a beautifully restored building at 4201 Main Street. It connects Houston’s talent, academic, corporate, and entrepreneurial communities in a collaborative environment designed to inspire and grow ideas. The Ion serves as a space where serendipitous collisions lead to meaningful connections and opportunities. What to Expect Coworking Spaces: Explore flexible work areas tailored for collaboration and creativity. Public Spaces: Discover spaces designed for networking, inspiration, and community events. Restaurants and Bars: Experience the on-site dining options that make the Ion a lively hub. Through its programs and partnerships, the Ion empowers individuals and organizations to build, solve, and innovate. From prototyping to pitching, accelerating to sustaining, the Ion is Houston’s headquarters for turning ideas into reality. Whether you have a promising product, an intriguing technology, a budding business idea, or are simply curious about the Ion and its offerings, this tour will provide valuable insights into how the Ion can help connect your “What-ifs” with “What now.” Don’t miss this opportunity to explore and be inspired by Houston’s innovation ecosystem.

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: SCORE Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

About SCORE Office Hours SCORE is the nation’s largest network of volunteer, expert business mentors. Since 1964, SCORE has helped more than 11 million entrepreneurs start, grow, or successfully exit a business. SCORE’s 10,000 volunteers provide free, expert mentoring, resources, and education in all 50 U.S. states and territories. Office Hours with SCORE take place each Friday morning. Additional partners will be on-site the second Friday of each month (excluding holidays)

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Why Networking Isn’t Turning Into Deals — And What To Do Instead
item.description:
Tuesday, July 14, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1991195256614?aff=oddtdtcreator Networking is one of the most common business development activities in oil & gas, energy, and industrial sectors. But for many companies, networking is not consistently turning into real opportunities. You’re attending events. You’re meeting people. You’re having conversations. You’re building relationships. But the pipeline does not always reflect it. This session is designed to break down why networking often fails to convert into deals—and what companies can do differently to turn conversations into qualified opportunities and real business development momentum. Because networking alone is not a strategy. A strong relationship can open the door, but without structure, follow-up, and clear movement, opportunities often stall before they ever become real pipeline. What You’ll Learn Why networking alone does not create predictable growth The difference between visibility, conversations, and actual pipeline Where most companies lose momentum after an event or introduction How to follow up in a way that creates real opportunities How to turn relationships into qualified conversations and deal flow What a simple system looks like for moving from connection to opportunity Who Should Attend Oil & gas operators and service companies Energy and industrial organizations Executives, business development leaders, and sales teams Founders and operators responsible for growth Companies that attend events, conferences, and industry gatherings but want better conversion from those conversations Event Format 30–40 minute presentation 15–20 minute discussion and Q&A Optional networking following the session About the Speaker Jada Powell is the Founder of Powell Consulting Group, where she works with oil & gas, energy, and industrial companies to build structured, repeatable business development systems that drive consistent pipeline and revenue growth. Her work focuses on helping mid-market companies move beyond relationship-driven growth into more scalable, measurable commercial strategies. This session is part of The Pipeline Series: How Oil & Gas Companies Actually Grow Revenue, a monthly in-person series at The Ion focused on helping oil & gas, energy, and industrial companies create more consistent pipeline, stronger follow-up, and better conversion from conversations to revenue.

From Ion District

Event details

Event details

item.title: Rice Tech Transfer Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, July 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Have an idea, startup, or company looking for innovative technology? Meet with the Rice University Office of Technology Transfer during office hours at The Ion to explore how university research can power your next breakthrough. Our team works with startups, entrepreneurs, and established companies to help commercialize cutting-edge technologies developed at Rice. During these office hours, you can: Discover Available Technologies – Learn about Rice innovations available for licensing across fields like health, energy, engineering, and computing. Understand the Licensing Process – Get a clear walkthrough of how to license university technology and what is required to move forward. Explore Collaboration Opportunities – Discuss potential partnerships with Rice researchers and ways to turn research into real-world products. Whether you’re building a startup, scouting new technology, or exploring research partnerships, stop by and connect with our team. Come with a question, leave with a path to commercialization. Stay connected and hear about new technologies available for licensing: https://mailchi.mp/rice/new-email-signup-page

From Ion District

Event details

item.description:
Friday, July 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

About SCORE Office Hours SCORE is the nation’s largest network of volunteer, expert business mentors. Since 1964, SCORE has helped more than 11 million entrepreneurs start, grow, or successfully exit a business. SCORE’s 10,000 volunteers provide free, expert mentoring, resources, and education in all 50 U.S. states and territories. Office Hours with SCORE take place each Friday morning. Additional partners will be on-site the second Friday of each month (excluding holidays)

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: NASA Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, July 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

NASA is bringing its expertise to Houston’s innovation hub! Every other Friday, join us for NASA’s office hours, designed to bridge the gap between Houston’s innovators, entrepreneurs, and startups with NASA’s elite experts, capabilities, and technology.  License the Future: Discover how to utilize NASA-developed technologies and intellectual property for your own projects. Partner for Growth: Explore how to grow your own goals and NASA’s mission together by leveraging unique human spaceflight capabilities and expertise from NASA Johnson Space Center. Build the Sector: Help us grow Houston’s commercial space ecosystem. Come with a vision, leave with a partnership. Just come walk up and say hello, or sign up in advance here.

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Houston Methodist Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, July 17, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Join us at the Houston Methodist Tech Hub at Ion on select Friday mornings for Office Hours to meet with the Center for Innovation team. Do you have an idea for an innovative healthcare solution? Do you work on technology in another industry that could be applicable to healthcare? Do you want to learn more about how hospital systems work with innovators? Come meet with us!

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Angel Investing 101
item.description:
Tuesday, July 21, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Ion – Conference Room 029 – 030, 4201 Main St, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

In a world where only the very wealthy, well connected, and major institutions have access to premium investment opportunities, which have created massive wealth for a small portion of the population, the Innovation Center for Urban Entrepreneurship team believes the key to closing income and wealth gaps globally is to democratize access to opportunities across the investment ecosystem. Join the Innovation Center for Urban Entrepreneurship team for an evening of learning, discussion and possibilities as we explore the basics of angel investing and the role it plays in bringing new products and services to market and the impact it has on wealth and job creation.. At this event, you’ll learn the key components to successfully navigate the critical-path of investing in private, early-stage companies. This session is ideal for the following investment related stakeholders: Prospective Angel Investors who want to learn more about the world of Angel Investing Investment Advisors (RIA/FA) who are looking for alternative investments for their clients Entrepreneurs who want to learn more about what Angels are looking for in their deals Entrepreneurs who want to meet/network with Angel Investors Ion District Investor Activation Program: The Houston Ion District Investor Activation Program along with Urban Capital Network is helping build an equitable climatetech industry capable of rapid deployment by addressing a fundamental challenge: the region’s lack of funding for early-stage climatetech startups, and especially for those led by underrepresented founders. This program from Greentown Labs and the Ion brings early-stage investors from Greater Houston—a region playing a pivotal role in the energy transition—into the fold, offering education on climatetech investing, channeling a pool of capital to young startups, and catalyzing a thriving climatetech investment ecosystem that prioritizes diversity, equity, and inclusion. If you would like to join the program, please fill out this form, and if you have additional questions, please contact Juliana Bernal Martinez at jbernal@greentownlabs.com.

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: NASA Tech Talks
item.description:
Thursday, July 23, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

We want HOU! Join us to discover NASA’s toughest tech challenges–and apply your skills and expertise to solve them.In these presentations, occurring the fourth Thursdays of the month, Mr. Montgomery Goforth and other aerospace subject matter experts will discuss the technology development challenges faced by NASA’s Johnson Space Center and the surrounding Aerospace community in our ongoing efforts as the hub of human spaceflight. Presentations will focus on the ways in which these challenges, and the associated opportunities, can be leveraged by Houston’s innovation community.Join us after the talk for drinks and networking at Second Draught!Past NASA Tech Talks have included: Montgomery B. Goforth discussing NASA Intellectual Property suitable for commercialization by small/start-up companies; Jared Daum discussing Orion’s parachutes and risk identification and mitigation that are showstoppers for the spaceflight parachute industry; Kevin Kobylka discussing current Guidance, Navigation, and Control (GNC) capabilities and needs; and Nick Skytland, Chief Technologist at the Johnson Space Center (JSC) and Director of the Business Development and Technology Integration Office. Future NASA Tech Talks will include: dual-use (space/terrestrial) technologies in the areas of: Avionics / Communication / Sensors Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for ultra-long lived wireless sensors Power Storage and Distribution Autonomous & Robotics Systems Extra-Vehicular Activity (EVA) Systems Virtual/Augmented Reality in Human Spaceflight Training & Execution Human Health & Performance / Human Systems Research About the Speakers Montgomery B. Goforth has more than 40 years of experience as both Engineer and Manager in a variety of highly technical space and defense-related efforts.  After working for a small defense contractor specializing in anti-submarine warfare, he joined NASA’s Johnson Space Center in 1990.  He has held leadership positions in multiple JSC Directorates and Program Offices. In his current role as Assistant Director of the JSC Engineering Directorate, he has established a framework to better focus Engineering’s efforts towards Human Spaceflight technology and systems development consistent with JSC and Agency goals for exploration. He has used this framework to guide Engineering’s internal investments and external partnerships supporting Lunar and Mars Exploration. Mr. Goforth was born in East Texas, raised in Southern California and Arizona, and graduated from Rice University (BSME 1982, MSME 1987). NASA Tech Talks topics include dual-use (space/terrestrial) technologies and focus areas in: Avionics / Communication / Sensors Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for ultra-long-lived wireless sensors Power Storage and Distribution Autonomous & Robotics Systems Extra-Vehicular Activity (EVA) Systems Virtual/Augmented Reality in Human Spaceflight Training & Execution Human Health & Performance / Human Systems Research NASA Intellectual Property suitable for commercialization by small/start-up companies Orion’s parachutes and risk identification and mitigation strategies Leveraging Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for ultra-long-lived wireless sensors

From Ion District
